title: Fast Food Drive-Thru Operations
des_qualification_note: Textbook DES. Discrete entities (cars/customers), discrete events (arrival, order, payment, pickup, exit), explicit simulated clock. Both typical criteria present: finite station capacity creates resource contention, and arrivals plus service times are stochastic. The walkthrough explicitly flagged drive-thru as a classic DES teaching domain.
summary_of_content_provenance: Rich topic with mixed-success fetch pattern. One first-party direct source (GitHub ZubairL SimPy drive-thru repo). Two second-party direct sources via search_snippet_aggregation: INFORMS Chick-fil-A teaching case article (paywalled, 403) and aggregated hybrid-ordering research from Tandfonline and ResearchGate summaries. Three paywalled/bot-blocked sources would live in a deferred human_followup_queue. Notable modeling feature: balking (customers leaving due to long queues) appears throughout the summary but has no structured representation in the current schema.
